title: Lot Divergence — Leakage Current Drift
description: Five production lots (A–E), 80 units per lot, leakage current measured every 3 months over a 24-month accelerated aging campaign. A reliability engineer suspects one lot may be drifting faster than the others but the visual difference is subtle and could be sampling noise.

ground_truth.note: Lot C has ~45% higher mean drift than the others.

suggested_questions: Is any lot aging faster than the others?
How confident can we be that the difference is real, not noise?
If Lot C continues at its current trajectory, when does it exceed the 4.0 μA spec limit?
